species: linc-ADAIN that is downregulated in white adipose tissue of obese humans.
tissue: linc-ADAIN that is downregulated in white adipose tissue of obese humans.
cell_type: its role in adipose inflammation and maturation of fat cells (adipocytes).
disease_state: linc-ADAIN is an anti-inflammatory lincRNA that limits adipose tissue expansion and lipid storage.
sample_source: RNA-seq of gluteal subcutaneous white adipose tissue from healthy, non-obese volunteers.
age: age 18–45), a National Institute of Health-sponsored protocol enrolled at University of Pennsylvania.
BMI: BMI ~21), as well as in abdominal subcutaneous and visceral omental adipose biopsies of obese subjects.
cell_line: hTERT adipocytes implanted into mice increases adipocyte size...
sex: with similar expression in sWAT and visceral adipose tissue (VAT) and slightly higher expression in males than in females.
